Title
Debug Tools Prompt

Purpose
Build a practical developer debug toolkit for Shadow Shift V2 so movement, combat, AI, rooms, and progression can be tested faster and more safely.

Why This Matters for V2
As system complexity grows, iteration speed depends on tooling. Good debug tools save enormous time and reduce regression risk.

Detailed Codex Instructions
Context:
The game already includes some testing and tuning needs across movement, enemies, progression, and rendering.

Goal:
Implement an in-game or developer-facing debug toolset that improves visibility and workflow.

Implementation Steps:
1. Audit the most common tuning and bug-fixing needs, such as:
   - invulnerability toggles
   - room warp
   - spawn enemy
   - grant abilities
   - show hitboxes
   - inspect AI states
   - refill health
2. Create a lightweight debug layer or overlay with keyboard shortcuts and optional on-screen information.
3. Make debug features easy to disable in normal play.
4. Keep debug logic organized and separate from release-facing UI where practical.

Constraints:
- Do not bury gameplay logic inside debug-only paths
- Keep tools lightweight and readable
- Preserve current architecture

Implementation Notes
- Debug tooling should expose state, not just mutate it.
- The best debug tools shorten validation loops for every future prompt.
- Consider routing debug actions through existing systems instead of bypassing them in dangerous ways.

Validation Checklist
- High-value debug actions are available
- Hitboxes/states can be inspected easily
- Room and progression testing is faster
- Debug code is organized
- Normal play is not polluted by debug-only behavior
